repo_prior_art.cite: pid_long_term_surrogate angle0: point-identification collapse under zero drift and exact terminal surrogacy

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyChettyImbensKang2019/2025 and Prentice1989: surrogate-index/no-drift surrogacy identification corner
ChenGengJia2007 and YinLiuGengLuo2020: observed-data surrogate paradox/sign-failure criteria, but not finite noisy proxy plus drift panel

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_long_term_surrogate angle0: closed-form binary transport endpoints/sign certificate

published_prior_art.cite: FanManzanaresParkQi2026 and MaYinLiuGeng2021 are adjacent; DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2023 covers generic discrete sharp bounds

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The exact-measurement/no-drift collapse is already the Prentice/Athey-Chetty-Imbens-Kang surrogate-index corner; keep it only as a sanity result.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses Yin, Liu, Geng, and Luo 2020, whose optimal observed-data criteria show same-observed-data surrogate paradox existence and are closer to the sign-ambiguity kernel than the cited individual-paradox paper alone.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=field as written: the margin boundary risks being generic sharp-set arithmetic unless it proves a nontrivial joint finite-proxy compatibility formula beyond Duarte et al.-style automated discrete partial identification.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem invokes only Assumptions randomization--bridge plus exact-corner, omitting Assumption drift; without Y_H(d)=m(U_J(d),X)+eps_d and eps_d=0 the collapse need not follow.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: B_K and R_rho are optimized as separate objects, but sign identification by inf B_K > R_rho requires a joint compatibility/Minkowski-sum theorem; outcome bounds and admissibility can make drift slack depend on the same m and latent law.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch says to separate the endpoint into bridge margin plus drift and paste endpoints, but that is exactly the hard sharpness step and no concrete algebraic identity establishes the claimed iff condition.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Yin, Liu, Geng, and Luo 2020 and Duarte et al. 2023/JASA to Related work, then state the precise epsilon-gap: finite noisy proxies plus bounded post-surrogate drift must yield a closed-form analytic boundary unavailable from generic observed-data surrogate-paradox criteria or automated bounds.

Replace Conjecture 2's separate B_K and R_rho condition with a joint feasible-set object, or explicitly conjecture and name the nontrivial theorem that the endpoint set equals B_K plus a symmetric residual interval under stated independence/pasting assumptions.

Sharpen Conjecture 1 to a minimal binary construction with a positive lower bound on the observed surrogate-index margin relative to eta, delta, and rho; otherwise the sign flip can look like a small-margin surrogate-paradox restatement.
Patch Theorem 1 by including Assumption drift in the hypotheses, or move it entirely into the sanity-check section as the exact-corner reduction rather than a main theorem.

recommended_next_step: Revise rather than pivot: fix Theorem 1's hypotheses, add the missed closest prior art, and restate Conjecture 2 as a genuinely joint finite-proxy sharp-bound theorem.
